top_signals.title: Hormuz Becomes a Holding Queue: Iran’s Toll Regime, Bilateral Carve-Outs, and a Bifurcating Strait
top_signals.summary: At a Glance Iran is moving to formalize a state-administered transit-toll regime under the new Persian Gulf Strait Authority (PGSA), with reported per-transit payments of up to $2 million settled in Chinese yuan and Bitcoin transfers to IRGC-linked wallets. Six India-flagged vessels transited inbound on May 18 as a coordinated cluster following bilateral engagement with ...
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: China keeps loan prime rate unchanged for 12th straight month in May
top_signals.summary: China kept its benchmark lending rates unchanged for a 12th straight month on Tuesday, as policymakers balanced the need to support a slowing economy against rising inflation risks. The People’s Bank of China left the one-year loan prime rate (LPR) at 3.00% and the five-year LPR at 3.50%, matching market expectations. Get premium insights on ...
top_signals.why_it_matters: Inflation expectations impacting rate path, bonds and global risk assets.

top_signals.title: Wah Kwong NatPower Backs Venture Energy and Guangzhou Port MoU on Zero-Carbon Shipping in Greater Bay Area
top_signals.summary: Wah Kwong NatPower Marine Ltd (WK NatPower), a joint venture between Wah Kwong Maritime Transport and NatPower Marine, is supporting a new initiative to develop zero-carbon shipping routes across China’s Greater Bay Area, following a recent broader Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) with Guangzhou Port Group. Under this MoU, WK NatPower will support maritime electrification by ...

top_signals.title: Japan’s trade surplus remained in the black in April as energy imports plunged
top_signals.summary: Exports remained robust, with chip exports rising 44% amid strong demand for artificial intelligence-related products.
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: Appalachian Region Could Hold Key to U.S. Battery Supply Chain Independence
top_signals.summary: The United States Geological Survey has made a potentially game-changing discovery in the Appalachian region. New research suggests that the ancient mountain range may be home to over 300 years' worth of lithium resources, presenting a potentially critical opportunity for the United States to wean itself off of international lithium deposits and build up domestic lithium markets. Sometimes referred to as 'white gold', lithium has become increasingly ubiquitous and indispensable in global technological and clean energy supply chains. Lithium-ion…
top_signals.why_it_matters: Commodity repricing impacting inflation expectations and industrial supply chains.

top_signals.title: China drives increased bauxite flows, but Guinea’s cutback could drag on growth
top_signals.summary: India has emerged as a key destination for bauxite as risks in the Arabian Gulf rise. Global bauxite flows increased by 24% y/y in April. Flows to China increased by 26% y/y. Flows to the UAE appear to have returned in April, but are 72% lower than the same period last year. India continues to ...
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Baltic Dry Index Falls for 4th Day
top_signals.summary: The Baltic Exchange’s dry bulk freight index, which monitors rates for ships carrying dry bulk commodities, declined for the fourth session on Wednesday, down 1.6% to its lowest since May 11 at 3,005 points. The capesize index, which typically transports 150,000-ton cargoes including iron ore and coal, decreased by 1.4% to 4,880 points; and the ...
top_signals.why_it_matters: Commodity repricing impacting inflation expectations and industrial supply chains.

top_signals.title: Ship Recycling Market Remains Subdued
top_signals.summary: The ship recycling market was still subdued during the past week. In its latest weekly report, Best Oasis (www.best-oasis.com), a leading cash buyer of ships said that “the Indian market has softened this week, with sentiment under pressure due to the rising exchange rate and a more cautious economic outlook. Buyers remain less inclined to ...

top_signals.title: Media Release “First dedicated STS standard for dry bulk released by INTERCARGO as operations increase”
top_signals.summary: Ship to ship (STS) transfers are an established part of dry bulk operations, and they are growing. As new trading areas and cargo flows emerge, STS operations are expanding into regions where port infrastructure or draft limitations make direct berth access impossible. However, the sector has been doing this without a dedicated standard. In tanker ...

top_signals.summary: Recent highlights Golar LNG Limited (“Golar” or “the Company”) reports Q1 2026 net income attributable to Golar of $84 million inclusive of $37 million of non-cash items1, Adjusted EBITDA1 of $106 million and Total Golar Cash1 of $1.0 billion.
